title: Liters to Gallons Converter
shortDescription: Convert liters to gallons with a direct conversion, clear decimal results, and practical real-world examples.
metaDescription: Convert liters to gallons with a direct factor and clear decimal results for cooking, fuel, tanks, and liquid planning.
intro: Enter a liter value and get the gallon equivalent immediately.
overview:
This converter changes liters into gallons using a fixed conversion factor, so you can compare volumes without guessing. It is useful when you work with recipes, fuel amounts, containers, or any measurement listed in liters but needed in gallons.

The result is shown as a decimal gallon value, which helps when you need an exact figure for planning or recordkeeping. If you are matching a tank specification, scaling a batch, or comparing product sizes, the conversion is handled in one step.
howItWorks: The tool applies the liters-to-gallons conversion factor.
Read the gallon result and, if needed, convert another value for comparison.

useCases.title: Recipe scaling

useCases.title: Fuel volume comparison

useCases.title: Production planning

examples.title: Fuel amount

examples.title: Water container

faq.question: Which gallon unit does this converter use?
faq.answer: It uses US gallons unless the page or local context explicitly states otherwise. A US gallon is smaller than an imperial gallon, so the result changes if you switch unit systems.

faq.question: Can I enter decimal liters?
faq.answer: Yes. Decimal values such as 2.75 liters are accepted, and the result is calculated from that exact input value.

faq.question: Why does the result show many decimal places?
faq.answer: Gallon values often do not land on a whole number. The decimal format preserves the actual converted volume instead of rounding too early.

faq.question: What mistake causes the wrong result most often?
faq.answer: The most common mistake is mixing US gallons with imperial gallons, or entering a value already written in gallons instead of liters.
